visa_free.notes: Up to 30 days per entry, up to 90 days per calendar year; tourism only. Presidential Decree No. 189/23 of 29 September 2023.

Up to 30 days per entry, up to 90 days per calendar year; tourism only. Presidential Decree No. 189/23 of 29 September 2023. The decree text reads 'Estados Unidos Independente de Samoa' (Independent State of Samoa, iso3=WSM). The SME FAQ page erroneously renders this as 'American Samoa'; the official decree and MIREX publication confirm the Independent State of Samoa.

e_visa.notes: Angola operates an official online e-visa (tourist visa) system via the SME portal (sme.gov.ao). Covered by Presidential Decree No. 189/23 of 29 September 2023. The tourism visa allows up to 30 days stay, can be extended once for an equal period. Applicants from nationalities not on the visa-free list must apply. Fee: 36,600 KZ paid in cash at the border. The portal requires a passport with at least 6 months validity, photograph, vaccination certificate, and return flight booking.

visa_policy.notes: Presidential Decree No. 189/23 of 29 September 2023 established a visa-free tourism regime for nationals of 98 listed countries for up to 30 days per entry and up to 90 days per calendar year. Five additional countries (Mozambique, Namibia, Sao Tome and Principe, South Africa, Zambia) are exempt under separate bilateral agreements. Data cross-referenced against official MIREX publications (brasil.mirex.gov.ao, africadosul.mirex.gov.ao) and governo.gov.ao presidential decree notices. Important: The official SME FAQ English page incorrectly renders 'American Samoa'; the actual decree text and MIREX publications list 'Estados Unidos Independente de Samoa' = Independent State of Samoa (WSM). The official SME page also contains minor typographical variants: 'Gracia' = Greece, 'Salmon Islands' = Solomon Islands, 'Papoa New Guinea' = Papua New Guinea, 'Saint Christopher of the Snows' = Saint Kitts and Nevis, 'Türkiye' = Turkey. The 98 countries break down as: Africa 14, Asia 11, Europe 35, Americas 8, Oceania 14, Caribbean/Pacific 16. The e-visa system at sme.gov.ao allows online pre-authorization for tourist visas (fee: 36,600 KZ paid in cash at border). All other visa categories (work, study, investor, temporary stay, residence) require consular or in-country application.

conditional_access.conditions: Acordo de Supressão de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 28.02.2003. Ordinary passport holders of South Africa are also visa-free under a separate bilateral tourism exemption agreement.
Acordo sobre Isenção de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, de Serviço e Ordinários, signed 07.10.2005. Covers all three passport types under this bilateral agreement.
Acordo sobre isenção recíproca de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, de Serviço, signed 21.09.2014. Ordinary passport holders also covered under separate bilateral agreement (17.11.2017).

Acordo sobre Supressão de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço ou Especial, signed 11.02.2015 in Kinshasa. Ordinary DRC passport holders are NOT visa-free and require a visa to enter Angola.

Acordo sobre isenção de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço (date not stated in MIREX document). Ordinary passport holders of Zambia are also visa-free under a separate bilateral tourism exemption.
Acordo sobre a Supressão de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed March 2012. Ordinary passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre supressão de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, Oficiais e ou de Serviço, signed 26.02.2013. Ordinary passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre supressão de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, Oficiais e ou de Serviço, signed 31.05.1999. Ordinary passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.

Acordo sobre isenção de vistos de Turismo em Passaportes Diplomáticos, Oficiais e ou de Serviço (date not stated in MIREX document). Ordinary Cuban passport holders are NOT on the visa-free list and require a visa to enter Angola.

Acordo sobre isenção de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, Oficiais e ou de Serviço (date not stated in MIREX document). Ordinary Ecuadorian passport holders are NOT on the visa-free list and require a visa to enter Angola.

Acordo de Supressão de vistos para titulares de Passaportes Diplomáticos, signed 03.02.2014. Covers diplomatic passports only (not service). Ordinary Spanish passport holders are also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.

Acordo sobre isenção recíproca de visto de Curta Permanência para portadores de Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 07.12.2013. Ordinary French passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.

Acordo sobre isenção recíproca de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 18.06.2012; additionally Acordo sobre Supressão de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 05.02.2013. Ordinary Italian passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre Supressão de vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 05.02.2013. Ordinary Portuguese passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.

Acordo sobre isenção recíproca de vistos para titulares de Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 05.02.2013. Ordinary Swiss passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre Supressão de Vistos, signed 26.02.1999. Ordinary Russian passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre Isenção de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 06.08.2009. Ordinary South Korean passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre Isenção Mútua de Vistos para titulares de Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 14.10.2014. Ordinary Chinese passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.
Acordo sobre Isenção de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed 03.04.2008. Ordinary Vietnamese passport holders are NOT on the visa-free list and require a visa to enter Angola.

Acordo sobre Isenção Mútua de Vistos para titulares de Passaportes Diplomáticos e de Serviço, signed Jakarta 11.04.2017. Ordinary Indonesian passport holders also visa-free under Presidential Decree No. 189/23.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Angolan government source. Angola does not operate a CBI scheme. No mention on sme.gov.ao, mirex.gov.ao, governo.gov.ao, or aipex.gov.ao.

programs.name: Investor Visa (Visto de Investidor)
programs.notes: The investor visa is granted by the Migration Authority (SME) to foreign investors or representatives of investing companies under Law No. 13/19 of 23 May (Legal System of Foreign Citizens, DP 13/19). It allows multiple entries and is valid for up to 2 years. After 3 years of uninterrupted stay, the holder may apply for a temporary residence permit. No minimum investment amount is stated on official source pages for the visa itself — any amount is eligible under the 2021 Private Investment Law (Law No. 10/21 of 22 April 2021). Application requires: criminal record certificate, medical certificate, passport copy (6 months validity), proof of investment registration (CRIP from AIPEX), two photographs, and proof of fee payment. Investor visas are obtained through Angolan consular missions. Source: Official Angola Consulate Dubai Investors & Tourism Guide (angolaconsulate.ae, official consulate domain).

programs.name: Residence Title (Titulo de Residencia)
programs.notes: The SME portal lists a 'Titulo de Residencia Prorrogacao' (Residence Title Extension) as an available service. A special CPLP-track residence title extension ('Titulo de Residencia Prorrogacao CPLP') is also available for nationals of Community of Portuguese-speaking Countries (CPLP) member states. Specific eligibility criteria and minimum stay requirements are behind the login portal and could not be retrieved from public-facing official pages.

validation_notes: CORRECTED (2026-06-13): Entry 'American Samoa' (iso3=null) has been corrected to 'Samoa' (Independent State of Samoa, iso3=WSM). The SME FAQ English page erroneously renders 'American Samoa', but the official decree text (Decreto Presidencial n.º 189/23) reads 'Estados Unidos Independente de Samoa' and the official MIREX publication at brasil.mirex.gov.ao explicitly lists 'Estados Unidos Independente de Samoa' — the sovereign Independent State of Samoa (WSM), not the US territory of American Samoa. Source: https://brasil.mirex.gov.ao/web/noticias/angola-isenta-cidadaos-de-98-paises-de-vistos-de-turismo and https://governo.gov.ao/noticias/1046/governo/decreto-presidencial/angola-isenta-cidadaos-de-98-paises-de-vistos-de-turismo.
VERIFIED (2026-06-13): The 98-country decree list count and all other entries verified against official MIREX and governo.gov.ao publications — no other errors found.
VERIFIED (2026-06-13): Solomon Islands (SLB) correctly appears in visa_free list. The SME FAQ page calls them 'Salmon Islands' but the decree text lists 'Ilhas Salomão' = Solomon Islands (SLB).
VERIFIED (2026-06-13): Haiti (HTI) correctly in visa_free list — confirmed by official MIREX as 'República do Haiti'.

VERIFIED (2026-06-13): No new presidential decree found expanding or modifying the 98-country list since September 2023. The Fragomen reference to 'Decree 880/2023' refers to the same October 9 effective date update of Decree 189/23; no separate decree expands the list.
VERIFIED (2026-06-13): CBI section confirmed as null — no citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Angolan government source.
VERIFIED (2026-06-13): e-visa fee confirmed at 36,600 KZ paid in cash at the border per official SME FAQ page.
gaps: The SME portal pages for individual visa categories (e-visa, residence title, work visa) are behind a login wall and detailed eligibility criteria could not be confirmed from the public-facing official pages.

Minimum investment amounts for the Investor Visa are not stated verbatim on the official source; the 2021 Private Investment Law (Law No. 10/21) states any investment amount is eligible, but no specific threshold appears in official sources.

Path-to-permanent-residency years not explicitly stated on official pages (after 3 years uninterrupted investor stay: mentioned in the official Angola Consulate Dubai guide, but 'temporary residence permit' rather than permanent residence is specified).
No official source confirms a digital nomad, startup, or highly-skilled worker fast-track visa program for Angola.

No official source confirms any freedom-of-movement agreement membership for Angola (SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons has not been ratified by Angola per available sources); agreements array left empty per strict official-source rule.

Niue is listed in the Presidential Decree as one of the 14 Oceania countries; it is a New Zealand-associated territory with no sovereign ISO-3 code under ISO 3166-1 (NU is its two-letter code but not alpha-3); retained as iso3=null.

No official Angolan source found granting conditional entry (visa-free/VoA/e-visa) based on holding a third-country visa (US, Schengen, UK, Canada, etc.), residence permit, or permanent residence card. Angola's entry rules are nationality-based, not credential-based.
No official source found for OCI or equivalent diaspora special-status entry rules for Angola.

The MIREX bilateral agreements document (angola.or.jp, dated 12 February 2019) may not reflect post-2019 agreements. Wikipedia notes pending (not-yet-in-force) agreements with Benin, Republic of the Congo, Djibouti, Ghana, Morocco and Timor-Leste for diplomatic passports — these were not confirmed on any official Angolan government source and are excluded per strict official-source rule.
Exact agreement dates for Cuba, Ecuador, and Zambia diplomatic/service passport waivers are not stated in the official MIREX document.

Angola–Congo Republic (Republic of the Congo, COG) diplomatic/service passport agreement details were found on angolex.com (non-official aggregator), not on an official .gov.ao domain; excluded from conditional_access per official-source rule — noted here as a gap.

outbound_official.note: No official Angola government outbound visa-list page located. The MIREX (Ministério das Relações Exteriores) portal at mirex.gov.ao and the SME (Serviço de Migração e Estrangeiros) at sme.gov.ao were checked exhaustively. The MIREX bilateral-agreements document (ucm.minfin.gov.ao PDF, hosted via angola.or.jp) lists bilateral visa-suppression/facilitation agreements but is framed as Angola granting inbound access to foreign nationals — it is not an outbound visa-free list for Angolan passport holders. No .gov.ao domain publishes a dedicated citizen-facing page (analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin) listing countries where Angolan ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. Rely on destination-side data.
